visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The scene opens with two young women standing in front of a Costco entrance. The woman on the left wears a black dress and holds a small prop microphone, while the woman on the right wears a beige cap and a white hoodie. Both are smiling and gesturing towards the camera against a backdrop of the Costco logo and blurred store interior. Bright flame-like graphic overlays frame the top and bottom. (2) A rapid sequence of close-up shots follows, displaying various products on store shelves or inside refrigerators. The camera remains mostly static for each shot, focusing on the packaging. Featured items include vacuum-packed A4 Japanese Wagyu beef, stacks of P&G air fresheners, and boxes of Tomica diapers. Each shot is centered and well-lit by bright supermarket lighting. (3) The montage continues with a high density of food items: Doritos snack boxes, Ocean Spray dried cranberries, and Iris Ohyama heat packs. The camera pans slightly across stacks of merchandise to show volume. Each product is accompanied by large, colorful text overlays showing pricing information. (4) Further products include Witor's chocolates, green bags of potato chips, Imei frozen strawberries, and fish snacks. The background varies between refrigerated sections and standard shelving units filled with bulk products. (5) The final sequence features household and beverage items like Evian water, sparkling water, Yakult drinks, and fresh bakery items like donuts and pastries. The video maintains a very fast pace with cuts happening roughly every 1.5 seconds, showcasing a wide variety of promotional items.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main female creators appear in the introduction.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: One woman has long dark hair and wears a black dress; the other wears a white cap and a white hoodie.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The video is filmed inside a large warehouse-style retail store (Costco), featuring industrial shelving, refrigerated display cases, and bulk product stacks.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with a high-energy, upbeat electronic pop track with a driving 4-on-the-floor beat. Two female voices speak simultaneously in an excited, high-pitched tone, welcoming viewers. A sharp 'ding' sound effect highlights the text appearing on screen. (2) As the video transitions to the product montage, a recurring 'pop' or 'ding' sound effect triggers with every visual cut. The narration becomes a rapid-fire delivery of product names and prices, maintaining a very high speech rate with almost no pauses. The background music continues at a steady 128 BPM, providing a rhythmic pulse that matches the editing pace. (3) Throughout the sequence of showing different foods and household items, the narrator's voice remains energetic and clear. Each new product slide is punctuated by a bright synthesizer chime. The balance between the loud, enthusiastic vocals and the slightly quieter background music is consistent, ensuring price details are audible. (4) The audio maintains this hyper-dense information flow until the end. The constant interplay of high-arousal speech, rhythmic electronic music, and repetitive technical sound effects creates a sense of urgency and excitement typical of promotional 'deal' videos. There is no significant ambient noise, indicating a polished post-production mix.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a promotional guide for Black Friday deals at Costco Taiwan. It aims to quickly inform viewers about significant discounts on a wide range of popular products, from high-end meat to daily household necessities, using a high-energy delivery style to encourage immediate shopping action.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video explicitly names products, shows price reductions, and uses slogans like 'Black Friday Special Report' while showcasing the Costco brand.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video utilizes a classic social media promotional format with high-density visual and audio information to maximize viewer retention and interest during a sales event.
